description: In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what is the caveat related to ACLs in a VSF stack?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers in HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.16.1006?
For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

description: The Client IP tracker tracks IP addresses until IP source lockdown is applied. After lockdown, only valid binding table entries are tracked. Already tracked IPs remain active through ARP probes, which use Layer 2 packets.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for Client IP Tracker?

Feature Caveat: Client IP Tracker - The Client IP tracker tracks IP addresses until IP source lockdown is applied. After lockdown, only valid binding table entries are tracked. Already tracked IPs remain active through ARP probes, which use Layer 2 packets.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for CMF?
CMF - No other checkpoint besides 'startup-configuration' gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

feature: HPE Aruba Networking CX Mobile App
description: VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for HPE Aruba Networking CX Mobile App?
Feature Caveat: HPE Aruba Networking CX Mobile App - VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

description: The switch may only forward packets at a rate of 100 packets per second (pps) if they trigger an ICMP redirect.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for ICMP Redirect?
Feature Caveat: ICMP Redirect - The switch may only forward packets at a rate of 100 packets per second (pps) if they trigger an ICMP redirect.

description: Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

description: The IP source-lockdown feature allows client traffic through the switch only if their MAC and IP addresses are in the binding database. VLAN-level configuration overrides port-level configuration only when the port belongs to a single VLAN; otherwise, VLAN-level configuration is not applied.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for IP Source Lockdown?

Feature Caveat: IP Source Lockdown - The IP source-lockdown feature allows client traffic through the switch only if their MAC and IP addresses are in the binding database. VLAN-level configuration overrides port-level configuration only when the port belongs to a single VLAN; otherwise, VLAN-level configuration is not applied.

description: MACsec policies with XPN cipher-suites is not supported on downlink ports on AOS-CX 6300 Switch series and 6200M switch models R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, and R8Q71A. MACsec policies should be configured with non-XPN cipher-suites to prevent traffic loss.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, for AOS-CX 6200 version 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - MACsec policies with XPN cipher-suites are not supported on downlink ports on AOS-CX 6300 Switch series and 6200M switch models R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, and R8Q71A. MACsec policies should be configured with non-XPN cipher-suites to prevent traffic loss.

description: MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module). NOTE MACsec is supported CX 6200M switch models only (R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, and R8Q71A, R8V08A, R8V09A, R8V10A, R8V11A, and R8V12A).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec in AOS-CX 10.16.1006?

MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module). MACsec is supported on CX 6200M switch models only (R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, and R8Q71A, R8V08A, R8V09A, R8V10A, R8V11A, and R8V12A).

description: When using Cisco AnyConnect as a dot1x supplicant, configure the CAK length to 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode. NOTE MACsec is supported CX 6200M switch models only (R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, and R8Q71A, R8V08A, R8V09A, R8V10A, R8V11A, and R8V12A).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, for AOS-CX 6200 version 10.16.1006, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - When using Cisco AnyConnect as a dot1x supplicant, configure the CAK length to 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode. NOTE MACsec is supported on CX 6200M switch models only (R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, and R8Q71A, R8V08A, R8V09A, R8V10A, R8V11A, and R8V12A).

description: Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. By default, AOS-CX uses the more secure cipher suite GCM-AES-XPN-256 for establishing MACsec secure links. However, Comware/PVOS does not support the XPN cipher suite.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. By default, AOS-CX uses the more secure cipher suite GCM-AES-XPN-256 for establishing MACsec secure links. However, Comware/PVOS does not support the XPN cipher suite.

description: MACsec uses a software-based implementation to track start and stop times for secure channels and secure associations. As the implementation is software-based, the stop times for MACsec secure channel and secure associations are only updated when they are deleted and therefore never updated in the output of the show macsec status detailed command. NOTE MACsec is supported CX 6200M switch models only (R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, and R8Q71A, R8V08A, R8V09A, R8V10A, R8V11A, and R8V12A).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, for AOS-CX 6200 version 10.16.1006, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - The implementation of MACsec on the CX 6200 switch models (R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, R8Q71A, R8V08A, R8V09A, R8V10A, R8V11A, and R8V12A) is software-based. As a result, the stop times for MACsec secure channels and associations are only updated when they are deleted, and thus never appear in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

description: In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends. NOTE MACsec is supported CX 6200M switch models only (R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, and R8Q71A, R8V08A, R8V09A, R8V10A, R8V11A, and R8V12A).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. NOTE MACsec is supported CX 6200M switch models only (R8Q67A, R8Q68A, R8Q69A, R8Q70A, and R8Q71A, R8V08A, R8V09A, R8V10A, R8V11A, and R8V12A).

description: OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what OSPF caveat is documented?
Feature Caveat: OSPF - OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

description: Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what RADIUS caveat is documented?
Feature Caveat: RADIUS - Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for REST?
REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for RIP/RIPng?
Feature Caveat: RIP/RIPng - RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what is the caveat related to SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: DHCP Snooping (v4/v6) should not be enabled on the UBT client VLANs in either vlan extend and local vlan mode.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for UBT/DHCP-Snooping?
Feature Caveat: UBT/DHCP-Snooping - DHCP Snooping (v4/v6) should not be enabled on the UBT client VLANs in either vlan extend and local vlan mode.

description: Use standalone or cluster-specific IP addresses for primary and backup controllers, ensuring they are not from the same cluster. For the primary controller use the IP address of a standalone controller or a controller in the primary cluster. For the backup controller use the IP address of a standalone controller or a controller in the backup cluster. Do not configure both primary and backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - Use standalone or cluster-specific IP addresses for primary and backup controllers, ensuring they are not from the same cluster. For the primary controller use the IP address of a standalone controller or a controller in the primary cluster. For the backup controller use the IP address of a standalone controller or a controller in the backup cluster. Do not configure both primary and backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster.

description: The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network admin should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what limitation is mentioned for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network admin should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

description: If there are license issues when onboarding a device to the primary or backup mobility conductor, the device will not switch to another conductor with an available license. If the primary conductor is unreachable, the device will tunnel to the backup conductor. Once the primary conductor is reachable again, the device will not automatically switch back. Administrators must manually disable and enable UBT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary conductor.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what additional caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - If there are license issues when onboarding a device to the primary or backup mobility conductor, the device will not switch to another conductor with an available license. If the primary conductor is unreachable, the device will tunnel to the backup conductor. Once the primary conductor is reachable again, the device will not automatically switch back. Administrators must manually disable and enable UBT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary conductor.

description: The 12-port 6200F switches (R8Q72A / R8V13A) do not support mixed VSF stacking.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for VSF?
Feature Caveat: VSF - The 12-port 6200F switches (R8Q72A / R8V13A) do not support mixed VSF stacking.

description: Only static VXLAN is supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - Only static VXLAN is supported.

description: Symptom: Traffic to the CPU will be adversely limited potentially below CoPP limits when the rate-limit unknown-unicast configuration element is added at a low rate. Scenario: The switch will not receive a portion of its CPU traffic. As a result, L2 learning, ARP resolution, route learning, and multicast learning will all be impacted. Workaround: Avoid using the rate-limit unknown-multicast configuration element, or raise the rate such that it does not interfere with CPU traffic.

description: Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in "device-mode" instead of "client-mode":{color:#000000}interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device{color}

description: Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal: 6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns.

description: Symptom: Network Management Systems does not display IPv4 addresses for Aruba CX switches running AOS-CX version 10.13. In AOS-CX 10.13, neither OID returns valid IPv4 data, resulting in incomplete device visibility. Workaround: N/A.

description: Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with the "snmp-server response-source" command prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file/fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing[snmp] srcipaddrentry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

description: Symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable Scenario 1: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 enabled and DHCPv6 disabled: Disabling DHCPv4 and enabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected When both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 disabled and DHCPv6 enabled: Enabling DHCPv4 and disabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Scenario 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected
symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable
scenario: 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected

description: Symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable Scenario 1: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 enabled and DHCPv6 disabled: Disabling DHCPv4 and enabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected When both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 disabled and DHCPv6 enabled: Enabling DHCPv4 and disabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Scenario 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected
symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable
scenario: 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected

description: Symptom: Traffic is dropped for a few seconds on a MACsec channel during a VSF switchover. Scenario: When the MACsec channel has data-delay protection enabled, there can be traffic drops for a few seconds on the channel post a VSF switchover due the reset of the MKA session on the interface. Workaround: Do not use data-delay protection in a MACsec policy if the system is deployed as a VSF stack.

description: Symptom: Traffic to the CPU will be adversely limited potentially below CoPP limits when the rate-limit unknown-unicast configuration element is added at a low rate. Scenario: The switch will not receive a portion of its CPU traffic. As a result, L2 learning, ARP resolution, route learning, and multicast learning will all be impacted. Workaround: Avoid using the rate-limit unknown-multicast configuration element, or raise the rate such that it does not interfere with CPU traffic.

description: Symptom: Switches are going out of sync with HPE ANW Aruba Central after firmware upgrades, with the switch failing to connect to HPE ANW Aruba Central. Scenario: This issue may occur after upgrading the firmware using HPE ANW Aruba Central, due to an internal race condition. Workaround: 1. Enable/Disable HPE ANW Aruba Central. 2. Remove and re-add the default route configuration.

description: Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal: 6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns.

description: Symptom: Network Management Systems does not display IPv4 addresses for Aruba CX switches running AOS-CX version 10.13. Scenario: This issue occurs when the NMS queries the switch using SNMP and relies on either of the following OIDs to retrieve IPv4 address information: 1.3.6.1.2.1.4.20.1.2(deprecated) or1.3.6.1.2.1.4.34.1.5(current but not accessible in 10.13). In AOS-CX 10.13, neither OID returns valid IPv4 data, resulting in incomplete device visibility. Workaround: N/A.

description: Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with the "snmp-server response-source" command prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file/fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing[snmp] srcipaddrentry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

description: Symptom: Traffic loss in an SVI with vsx active-forwarding enabled, when vsx active-forwarding is disabled/removed on a entirely different SVI. Scenario: This issue can occur in a configuration where VSX active-forwarding is enabled, Workaround: There are two known workarounds to fix the traffic loss. Unconfigure and reconfigure vsx active-forwarding in the SVI, or save the config and reboot the VSX peers.
